content.text: {"dimension":"ui-ux","applicable":true,"notApplicableReason":"","summary":"Reviewed the diff for UI/UX concerns. The only user-facing UI change is `wwwroot/index.html`, rewritten from a static 'Hello World!' page into a minimal SPA shell that mounts the app via `dist/app.js`. The markup is otherwise solid: it correctly declares `lang="en"`, adds a viewport meta, sets a meaningful ``, and uses a module script + stylesheet link. The remainder of the diff (DI registration, static-file/fallback middleware, launch settings, csproj, appsettings) has no UI surface. Two real findings apply to the index.html change: a missing `<noscript>` fallback (so JS-disabled users see a blank page) and a blank initial render with no loading/skeleton state (perceived-performance/layout-shift polish).","findings":[{"id":"missing-noscript-fallback","severity":"medium","file":"wwwroot/index.html:9-11","title":"JS-only SPA provides no <noscript> fallback for users without JavaScript","description":"The new index.html renders an empty `<div id="app"></div>` and depends entirely on `<script type="module" src="dist/app.js">` to populate the UI. If JavaScript is disabled, blocked, or fails to load/parse (e.g., a build artifact is missing, a CSP blocks modules, or the user is on an assistive-tech environment with scripts turned off), the page is completely blank with no explanation. This is a real accessibility/usability defect: affected users cannot tell why the screen is empty or that the app requires JavaScript. The fix is to add a `<noscript>` element inside `<body>` with a brief, actionable message telling the user the app requires JavaScript to function.","fixPrompt":"In the file `wwwroot/index.html`, add a `<noscript>` fallback inside the `<body>` element so that users with JavaScript disabled see an explanatory message instead of a blank page. Immediately after the `<div id="app"></div>` line (and before or after the `<script>` tag), insert: <noscript> <p style="color: #e6e6e6; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; padding: 16px;">File Browser requires JavaScript to run. Please enable JavaScript in your browser settings and reload the page.</p> </noscript> Keep the existing markup, viewport meta, charset, title, stylesheet link, and module script unchanged. The message should be plain text (no interactive controls) and styled to be visible against the dark background defined in `dist/app.css`."},{"id":"blank-initial-render-no-loading-state","severity":"low","file":"wwwroot/index.html:9-10","title":"Empty #app container shows nothing while the module script loads","description":"The page renders an empty `<div id="app"></div>` and only populates content once `dist/app.js` is fetched, parsed, executed, and any initial API request completes. During that window the user sees a bare dark background with no visual indication that the app is loading or that anything is happening. On slower connections or cold loads this reads as a broken/blank page and can cause a visible flash when content suddenly appears. Adding a lightweight in-document loading placeholder (e.g., a 'Loading…' text node inside #app that the script replaces on mount, or a simple skeleton) improves perceived performance and reduces layout shift.","fixPrompt":"In the file `wwwroot/index.html`, provide a lightweight initial loading indicator inside the `<div id="app">` container so the page is not blank before the module script mounts. Replace the empty `<div id="app"></div>` with something like: <div id="app"> <p style="color: #9a9a9a; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; padding: 16px;">Loading File Browser…</p> </div> The text must be a child of `#app` so that the existing app bootstrap (which clears `#app` via `root.innerHTML = ''` before rendering) automatically removes it once the app mounts. Do not add any JavaScript for this — it must work from static HTML. Keep all other markup (charset, viewport, title, stylesheet link, module script, lang attribute) unchanged."}]}
